concerns.text: SC-005 sets a target of '≥ 90% successful compilations' but the spec is a planning document. Per the 'Sanctioned [deferred] markers' rule and the SC preamble, specific empirical thresholds (like 90%) must be deferred to the research phase. This SC is currently unmeasurable because the target is arbitrary and not derived from a baseline or hypothesis in this stage.

SC-003 states coverage is measured against 'the expected range defined in the research hypothesis.' While the 'Expected results' section in idea.md mentions 40-60%, the spec itself does not explicitly define this range as the reference for SC-003. A success criterion must be self-contained or explicitly link to a defined reference value within the spec to be verifiable without cross-referencing external documents.

FR-007 requires limiting the sample size to 'a feasible subset' to meet a 6-hour limit. 'Feasible' is not a verifiable condition. The requirement must specify the exact mechanism for determining this limit (e.g., 'System MUST stop sampling after 50 items' or 'System MUST stop when execution time exceeds 5 hours') to be testable.

US-3 AC-2 requires the report to state if coverage achieved '≥ 40% of the manual baseline coverage.' This hardcodes a specific empirical threshold (40%) into the functional requirement. Per the spec's own preamble on Success Criteria, planning docs should defer specific values. This makes the FR untestable against a variable hypothesis and violates the [deferred] convention.
